term_inventory_hebrew.meaning: Super abundance of fat, excessive fat.
Flesh. Aramaic equivalent of ba.sar (H1320). Verse set shared with ba.sar.

1) flesh: 1a) of the body — 1a1) of humans, 1a2) of animals; 1b) the body itself; 1c) male organ of generation (euphemism); 1d) kindred, blood-relations; 1e) flesh as frail or erring (man against God); 1f) all living things; 1g) animals; 1h) mankind. Aramaic equivalent: be.shar (H1321). Verse set shared with be.shar.

1) flesh, food, body, near kin, near kinswoman: 1a) flesh — 1a1) as food, 1a2) for physical power (figurative); 1b) flesh relation, blood relation; 1c) self. A secondary sub-sense 'kinswomen' is listed as H7608 (sha.a.rah).
1) intestines, bowels — 1a) meaning uncertain; 2) (CLBL) food, something eaten.

1) to go out, come out, exit, go forth: 1a) (Qal) — 1a1) to go or come out or forth, depart; 1a2) to go forth to a place; 1a3) to go forward, proceed; 1a4) to come or go forth with purpose or result; 1a5) to come out of; 1b) (Hiphil) — 1b1) to cause to go or come out, bring out, lead out; 1b2) to bring out of; 1b3) to lead out; 1b4) to deliver; 1c) (Hophal) to be brought out or forth. Note: the 1066 occurrences span all sub-glosses; flesh-relevant verses are not separately identified in STEP.
